headlines.title: Artemis crew home safely after completing historic mission to the Moon
headlines.description: Four astronauts splashed down in the Pacific after a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans.

headlines.title: Lyse Doucet: Historic US-Iran talks must bridge deep distrust
headlines.description: Face-to-face talks would mark the highest-level engagement between Iran and the US since the 1979 Islamic Revolution.

headlines.title: UK shelves Chagos deal after Trump opposition
headlines.description: UK government officials say they are not entirely abandoning the agreement but have run out of time.

headlines.title: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity Sentebale he co-founded
headlines.description: Prince Harry left the charity last year in an acrimonious dispute over how Sentebale was being managed.

headlines.title: Final push for votes as challenger to Hungary's Orbán senses victory
headlines.description: As Péter Magyar's opposition movement leads in the polls, tens of thousands of anti-Orbán supporters fill Heroes' Square in Budapest.

headlines.title: Back to Earth: What happens to the Artemis II astronauts now?
headlines.description: The astronauts will have medical checks and will be reunited with their families.

headlines.title: Artemis II mission was a triumph. Now comes the hard part
headlines.description: The mission was almost flawless but there are considerable obstacles ahead before a Moon landing.

headlines.title: I got a £10,000 loan for my nursing degree. Now they say it's an error and I have to pay it back
headlines.description: Students and graduates have described their shock at being told they were not eligible for the loans they received.

headlines.title: Your guide to the UK's music festival season for summer 2026
headlines.description: Glastonbury is having a fallow year but there are huge line-ups at many other festivals this year - and something different if you fancy that too.

headlines.title: The parents using play to stop children getting 'trapped' by screens
headlines.description: Researchers say more speech and interaction around children can aid the development of young brains.

headlines.title: Hundreds contact BBC about mystery skin condition 'hell' - but doctors can't agree it exists
headlines.description: Some think it's a severe case of eczema. Others say it's a condition called TSW. But doctors are stuck in a dilemma.

headlines.title: This idyllic US town was full of police families - and a serial killer in their midst
headlines.description: For years, rumours swirled about who could be behind the bodies that washed up on the shores of Gilgo Beach.

headlines.title: Concerns after satellite provider restricts Iran images following US pressure
headlines.description: Planet Labs says it is restricting coverage of Iran and parts of the Middle East indefinitely.

headlines.title: The five big sticking points in US-Iran talks

headlines.title: Tankers urged not to pay toll to Iran for use of strait
headlines.description: Tanker firms are being advised not to pay Iran any levies in return for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.title: Is a 'Super El Niño' on the way and how will it affect the UK?
headlines.description: El Niño is expected to develop later this year, but how strong will it be and what will this mean for the weather in the UK and around the world?

headlines.title: Melania Trump's speech propels Epstein crisis back to forefront
headlines.description: She has now placed herself squarely into the Epstein story and at odds with the administration, which wants to end the investigation.

headlines.title: Energy bills are set to rise - but not just due to the Iran war
headlines.description: While the world is gripped by an energy crisis, Britain is facing another key driver of higher costs.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'Harry sued' and Vance warns Iran 'don't try to play us'
headlines.description: News of the Duke of Sussex being sued for defamation by a charity he co-founded dominates several of Saturday's front pages.

headlines.title: He lives on through his work - 16 incredible photos from late Planet Earth cameraman

headlines.title: British man, 77, dies after tourist bus crashes in Canary Islands

headlines.title: Epstein survivors' calls to meet King and Queen harder to ignore as US visit approaches

headlines.title: Watch: West Ham thrash Wolves to move out of relegation zone
headlines.description: Taty Castellanos and Konstantinos Mavropanos both score twice as West Ham beat Wolves to move out of the relegation zone in the Premier League.

headlines.title: Trump administration unveils 250-foot 'Triumphal Arch' design
headlines.description: The new arch, if approved, would be taller than the US Capitol building and the Lincoln Memorial.

headlines.title: EU fingerprint and photo travel rules come into force

headlines.title: Man arrested after baby girl dies from dog bite

headlines.title: Dart and Burrage send GB to BJK Cup finals
headlines.description: Harriet Dart and Jodie Burrage win the decisive doubles match in straight-sets to send Great Britain through to September's Billie Jean King Cup finals with an impressive 3-0 win against Australia.

headlines.title: 'Light and lean' Fury shows intent with weigh-in
headlines.description: Tyson Fury says he took his undefeated record "for granted" as he appeared lean and sharp at the weigh-in for Saturday's bout with Arslanbek Makhmudov.
